15_Supplement_Safety_and_Interaction_Eng: Recommend checking any supplement (especially pre-workouts and "proprietary blends") against a batch-tested/certified list (e.g., Informed Sport-style certification) before use — system does not maintain a live banned-substance database and must say so rather than imply completeness.
Never assume Tier 1 supplements are automatically competition-safe; contamination risk exists even for basic protein/creatine products from non-certified manufacturers.
Vegetarian/vegan protein sourcing common — default to noting protein-per-serving and completeness (combine plant sources) rather than assuming whey by default.
Heat/humidity climate consideration — electrolyte emphasis higher priority than in temperate-climate default guidance (interacts with file 13 Section 9 environmental flags).

Unregulated local market products more common — Section 3's proprietary-blend caution applies with extra emphasis; recommend FSSAI-labeled products with transparent ingredient lists where possible, without endorsing specific brands.
